meta.title: Services
meta.description: Four packaging and print disciplines, from Stockholm to twelve countries. Gift boxes, paper bags, corporate print and bespoke production for premium brands.

hero.heading: Four disciplines, one language.
hero.description: We work across four connected areas — rigid boxes, paper bags, corporate print and bespoke projects. Every engagement is treated with the same demand on material, construction and delivery. Choose a discipline for full scope, spec and lead time.

cta.heading: Brief in motion?
cta.description: Send us scope and brief and we'll come back with a proposal within 48 hours — no boilerplate pitch.

boxes.name: Gift boxes
boxes.description: Die-cut premium boxes with foil, embossing, and interior detailing for luxury brands.

meta.title: Gift boxes
meta.description: Die-cut premium gift boxes with foil, embossing and interior construction. FSC-certified board, lithographic printing, QC in Stockholm.

hero.heading: Boxes that hold the moment of first contact.
hero.tagline: We design and produce gift boxes for launches, limited editions and premium lines — with unboxing, construction and warehouse logic held in the same spec.

types.heading: Four box types we produce.
types.description: Every box type has its place — we match construction to the unboxing intent and the storage volume, not the other way around.

rigid.title: Rigid box
rigid.description: Glued rigid board with high stiffness for premium launches and gifts. Board 1.5–3 mm; add a magnetic closure or interior foam bed for ceremonial unboxing.

folding.title: Folding carton
folding.description: Die-cut, auto-glued carton for series production from 500 units. The right call for retail volume and shipping — lithographic print, embossed logotype, matte lamination as a default.

sleeve.title: Sleeve pack
sleeve.description: Two-part construction — inner tray plus a sliding sleeve. Used when the interior surface is meant to carry brand, product, or a message that only reveals after the first motion.

magnetic.title: Magnetic closure
magnetic.description: Rigid box with flush-fit magnets in lid and base. Provides the sound-controlled closure premium brands use to signal quality. Tested against our own in-house load standard.

materials.heading: Specs we work in.
materials.description: FSC-certified board is the starting point. From there we pick weight and grade by construction need and run size — no unnecessary upgrades.

board.title: Board & grade
board.description: From solid bleached board (SBS) for luxury tier to recycled kraft for sustainability-led lines.

coating.title: Surface finish
coating.description: Lamination or UV varnish shapes the first tactile meeting. We always proof on the actual board before sign-off.

decoration.title: Decoration & emboss
decoration.description: Foil, emboss and die-cut details are used sparingly — where they make the logotype more felt, not louder.

closure.title: Closure & inserts
closure.description: Interior construction belongs to the spec — not as an afterthought. We spec magnets, satin ribbon or foam bed at concept stage.

cases.heading: Boxes in the wild.
cases.description: A selection of cases where the box construction carried the launch.

meridian.label: Meridian Liquid Labs
meridian.summary: Signature box for a limited-edition launch with magnetic closure and holographic foil.

lund.label: Lund Kosmetik
lund.summary: Presentation kit in matching quality — rigid board, satin ribbon, interior foam bed.

cta.heading: Time to build the box?
cta.description: Send dimensions, volume and deadline — we'll come back with construction, material and price within 48 hours.

bags.name: Paper bags
bags.description: Sturdy carry bags with cotton handles, matte lamination, and embossing — retail and gift-ready.

meta.title: Paper bags
meta.description: Sturdy paper bags for retail, gifting and events. Matte lamination, hot-foil, cotton or satin handles — produced with FSC paper.

hero.heading: Bags that carry the brand past checkout.
hero.tagline: From laminated luxury shopping bags to embossed kraft carriers — we build paper bags that survive store traffic, weather and lighting, without the brand falling off along the way.

types.heading: Four bag types for four carry needs.
types.description: We pick paper weight and handle by what is going into the bag — not by a standard size in a catalogue.

luxuryShopping.title: Laminated luxury shopping bag
luxuryShopping.description: Matte or soft-touch laminated kraft board with hot-foil and cotton handles. Reinforced top and base — built for repeat use and to hang in the customer's hallway.

gift.title: Gift bag
gift.description: Narrower format with satin ribbon or grosgrain loops. Used as the primary wrap for a single product — not a shopping bag with a logo on it.

kraft.title: Kraft bag, sustainability profile
kraft.description: Unbleached or lightly tinted kraft, recycled when possible, with twisted paper handles. The right call when recycled material is part of the brand promise.

eventRetail.title: Event & retail volume
eventRetail.description: Paper bags for launches, trade shows and campaigns — produced at higher volume (5,000+), to the same QC standard as our premium line.

materials.heading: Specs we work in.
materials.description: Paper weight carries the load; the handle carries the feel. We pick both together, never separately.

paper.title: Paper & weight
paper.description: Kraft paper from 120 to 210 gsm. White, unbleached or tinted — always with a surface that keeps print legible.

handle.title: Handles
handle.description: The handle is what sticks in memory. We choose material by carried weight, brand tone and reuse rate.

finish.title: Surface finish
finish.description: Lamination protects and controls gloss level. Soft-touch is only applied where we have tactile-tested the result.

print.title: Print & decoration
print.description: Offset for multi-colour, silkscreen for specials, foil for lines meant to be felt with a finger.

cases.heading: Bags in the wild.
cases.description: A selection of cases where paper weight and handle mattered as much as the print.

veka.label: Veka Stockholm
veka.summary: Holiday collection with hot-foil, matte lamination and cotton handles. 8,000 units.

meridian.label: Meridian Retail
meridian.summary: Retail carry for a limited launch, sharing the visual language of the box.

cta.heading: Shall we build the bag?
cta.description: Send dimensions, paper weight, handle choice and volume — we'll come back with a proposal within 48 hours.

corporate.name: Corporate print
corporate.description: Business cards, presentation folders, and brand collateral in matching quality.

meta.title: Corporate print
meta.description: Internal use, external impression. Brochures, presentation folders, notebooks and event material in the same premium finish as our packaging lines.

hero.heading: For internal use, for your brand.
hero.tagline: Brochures, presentation folders, notebooks and stationery — produced to the same quality as our packaging lines, but built for internal volume, sales use and event rollouts.

products.heading: Five product categories.
products.description: The selection covers the printed items companies use most internally and in sales contexts. We say no to one-off business-card jobs and flyers — they don't belong here.

brochures.title: Brochures & reports
brochures.description: A5–A4, saddle-stitched or perfect-bound, 4-colour offset. Typical volume 500–5,000. Used for annual reports, product catalogues or investor material.

folders.title: Presentation folders
folders.description: Die-cut folders with pocket, business-card slit and embossed logotype. Built for sales meetings and onboarding kits, often in sets with brochure and USB insert.

notebooks.title: Notebooks & journals
notebooks.description: Perfect-bound or wire-O, with emboss or foiled cover. Ruled, dotted or plain — produced for internal use, client gifting or onboarding bundles.

stationery.title: Letterheads & envelopes
stationery.description: Letterheads, business cards and envelopes in matching grade. FSC paper as a standard, with or without embossed logotype.

event.title: Event & trade-show material
event.description: Roll-ups, signage, programmes and badges for launches and events. Short lead time — fixed 10 working-day production window.

specs.heading: Specs we work in.
specs.description: Corporate print is not a downgraded copy of the packaging spec — same grade, same QC, same presses.

paper.title: Paper & grade
paper.description: FSC-certified paper as a base. Weight is driven by binding and volume.

finish.title: Finish & decoration
finish.description: Premium finish isn't an upsell — it's part of the base spec. Foil and emboss go where the brand mark earns it.

binding.title: Binding
binding.description: Choice is driven by use pattern — an annual report and a notebook shouldn't be bound the same way.

volume.heading: Volume with premium finish, not luxury veneer.
volume.description: We produce in runs from 250 to 25,000+. Per-unit price drops with volume — the spec doesn't. Call us for a project-based quote.

small.label: Short run
small.note: Launches, events, limited campaigns. 10–15 working days lead time.

medium.label: Standard volume
medium.note: Sales material, onboarding kits and yearbooks. 15–20 working days with two press proofs.

large.label: Large series
large.note: Internal use, global rollouts, retail campaigns. Quote-based lead time — we plan the production together.

volume.disclaimer: Pricing is confirmed after signed spec and press proof — there is no fixed catalogue.

cta.heading: Shall we take your corporate print to the same level?
cta.description: Send material spec and volume — we'll come back with production and pricing within 48 hours.

custom.name: A++ Custom production
custom.description: Bespoke packaging builds — limited editions and flagship showcase projects.

meta.title: A++ Custom production
meta.description: Flagship projects, PR kits, influencer seeding, retail launches. Call first, brief second — for projects that don't fit a standard quote.

hero.heading: Projects that need something other.
hero.tagline: Our bespoke track is reserved for projects where the standard process isn't enough — limited launches, PR boxes, event and seeding kits built from first sketch to delivered pallet with a single project owner.

body.pitch: Bespoke projects start with a call, not a form. Thirty minutes on the phone is enough for us to say whether we're the right fit — and that matters more than a long presentation.
body.terms: We take no more than eight bespoke projects per year. Each one gets a dedicated project owner from concept to signed master, with no handovers.
body.close: If we say yes, we write an engagement letter with timeline, payment model and deliverables. If we say no, we send a referral within 24 hours.

examples.heading: Five project types we often land in.
examples.description: This list is not a menu — it indicates what we'll say yes to. Every project is scoped from zero.

limitedEdition.title: Limited edition launch kit
limitedEdition.description: Capped run (100–2,000 units) that signals exclusivity. Rigid box, numbered labels, interior ritual. Built for launch events and media sampling.

prKits.title: PR box & press kit
prKits.description: For flagship launches and product introductions — a box opened at a desk or on camera. Construction built for photo and video moments.

seeding.title: Influencer seeding kit
seeding.description: Smaller runs (50–500), higher visual spend per unit. Often personalised labels and hand-packed inserts. Delivered with logistics direct to recipient.

eventKits.title: Event & exhibition kit
eventKits.description: Booth and stage material for launch events, press rooms and galleries. Box, print, signage and transport instructions for travelling formats.

flagship.title: Flagship & showcase projects
flagship.description: Projects meant for trade fair, office display or portfolio. We build both production-run and display pieces in the same engagement.

cta.heading: Have something that doesn't fit a standard quote?
cta.description: Call us directly — we'd rather talk before either of us spends time on a brief. If it's a good fit, we write an engagement letter within a week.

hero.headline: Premium packaging for brands that mean something.
hero.subtitle: From Stockholm to twelve countries. We design and produce gift boxes, paper bags, and corporate print for premium and luxury brands.

services.heading: Four disciplines, one language.
services.description: We work across four main disciplines. Every engagement blends material sensibility, construction, and production — from sketch to palletised delivery.

portfolio.heading: Recent projects.
portfolio.description: A selection of work where material, construction, and brand meet.

items.meridian: Signature box for a limited-edition launch.
items.veka: Holiday paper bags with foil and cotton handles.

cta.heading: Ready to make something memorable?
cta.description: Tell us about the brief — we'll come back with a proposal within 48 hours.

meta.description: Selected packaging and print projects by AW AB — gift boxes, paper bags, corporate print and bespoke production for premium brands.

hero.heading: Projects that carry brands.
hero.description: A selection of engagements where material, construction and brand met. Filter by discipline — or browse the full archive.

meta.title: About
meta.description: AW AB is a Swedish packaging agency based in Stockholm, founded in 2019. We design and produce gift boxes, paper bags, and corporate print for premium and luxury brands.

hero.heading: Packaging is the first physical meeting with your brand.
hero.description: We design and produce for brands that need that moment to hold — from the first impression on the shop floor to the back of the carton in the warehouse.

body.founding: AW AB was founded in 2019 by a team with backgrounds in both graphic design and industrial packaging production. The aim was to close the gap between a brand's visual language and the physical object that carries it — a gap we saw recur in projects we'd previously done separately.
body.growth: Today we run a twelve-person studio working with Nordic premium brands, international luxury labels, and selected corporate clients who pack in larger volume. Production happens with carefully selected partners in Sweden, Germany, and Italy, and every proof is checked in our own studio before it leaves the building.
body.ethos: We believe packaging is a design discipline in its own right — not an afterthought to a brand book. That shapes how we read briefs, how we price, and how we plan time.

values.heading: Four commitments we don't negotiate.
values.description: These aren't marketing phrases. They're what drives a yes and a no on every engagement.

quality.name: Quality without compromise
quality.description: Every product goes through internal QA before it leaves the studio. Material samples and press proofs are part of the standard process, not a premium add-on.

sustainability.name: Sustainability by default
sustainability.description: FSC-certified paper as a starting point. We always propose the minimum material spec that meets the design intent — even when the client hasn't asked.

precision.name: Creative precision
precision.description: Technical drawings follow concepts, not the other way around. Design dictates construction — not the reverse. That's why we have designers and production engineers in the same studio.

partnership.name: Discreet partnership
partnership.description: We don't publish client names without written consent. Project information stays internal until the client decides it can be shared.

process.heading: From brief to delivery, in six steps.
process.description: Our engagements follow a defined process. Each step has a clear entry condition and sign-off before the next begins.

brief.title: Brief & research
brief.description: Brand audit, material studies, and technical feasibility. We ask for things others don't — and say no early if we don't see fit.

concept.title: Concept & prototype
concept.description: Two or three directions with physical prototypes, not renders. You should be able to pick the finished object out of a bag before we sign.

production.title: Production & delivery
production.description: Proofing, certified presses, QC against a signed master. Freight logistics are always included.

team.heading: The twelve people behind AW AB.
team.description: A full team presentation is on the way. Until then: we are a team of twelve — designers, production engineers, and project leads — based in a studio in Gamla stan, Stockholm.

cta.heading: Brief in motion?
cta.description: We review brief and scope before we respond — no boilerplate pitch, no spam.

meta.title: Process
meta.description: How AW AB works — five steps from brief to delivered pallet. Typical lead time 8–14 weeks. FAQ on lead time, volume, certifications, NDAs and pricing.

hero.heading: From idea to delivery.
hero.description: We work in five defined steps — each with a clear entry condition and a sign-off before the next begins. No part of the process is ad-hoc. That is how we hold timeline, spec and price.

brief.title: Brief & Discovery
brief.description: We open with a scoping session — either on-site or over call. We want to see the product, read the brand book, and understand both the in-store moment and the warehouse constraints before we talk construction. We say no early if we don't see a fit.

concept.title: Concept & Design
concept.description: We present two or three concept directions. Each includes a mood board, physical material samples, and a technical sketch of the construction. No generic renders — we put real paper on the table so you can feel the decision before taking it.

prototype.title: Prototype & Approval
prototype.description: One physical prototype is hand-built and shipped to your office. We also run certified press proofs for colour-critical specs. Nothing goes to production before a signed master prototype sits in your building.

production.title: Production
production.description: Production runs on certified presses at partner facilities in Sweden, Germany and Italy. Every shift is logged; we attend press set-up and the first finished unit of each SKU. Random QC samples pulled through the run are compared against the signed master.
deliverables: Final quality control against signed master

delivery.title: Delivery & Follow-up
delivery.description: Freight and logistics are part of the deliverable — palletising, customs and warehouse drop-off are planned in step 01, not scrambled at the end. We follow up two weeks after delivery to confirm what ended up in store, in warehouse and in use.

faq.heading: What procurement usually asks first.
faq.description: Eight questions that typically come up during scoping — lead time, volume, certifications, NDA, pricing. If yours isn't here, call direct.

duration.a: Standard is 8–14 weeks from signed spec to delivered pallet, depending on quantity and materials. Bespoke projects and custom kits take 12–16 weeks. We build buffer into the timeline from the start — not as a correction when something slips.

volume.q: What quantities do you work with?
volume.a: From 100 units for limited editions and custom kits, up to 50,000+ for retail volume. Below 100 we usually say no or refer elsewhere — set-up costs don't amortise at that scale. No upper cap.

shipping.a: Yes. We ship within the EU, to the US, the UK and Asia. Customs documents, pallet marking and consolidation are part of the quote — no hidden freight-forwarder lines added at the end.

certifications.q: What certifications do your materials carry?
certifications.a: FSC Mix as the baseline for all paper and board. Selected lines available as FSC Recycled or 100% recycled. On request: ISEGA (food contact), EU REACH-compliant inks. Certificates are delivered per batch.

prototyping.q: How does the prototype process work?
prototyping.a: One hand-built prototype is delivered to your office in step 03. Two revision rounds are included; additional rounds are billed hourly. Press proofs for colour-critical specs run on a certified proof press — never a digital simulation.

nda.a: Yes, we sign a mutual NDA before the brief. Our standard template is available on request; your own template is reviewed within two business days. Project information stays internal until you explicitly approve publication.

revisions.q: How many revision rounds are included?
revisions.a: Two concept revisions in step 02 and two prototype revisions in step 03 are included in the base quote. Additional rounds can be added before the contract is signed — we always state round count and hourly rate in the quote, never after the fact.

pricing.a: Fixed quote based on signed spec. No hourly billing, no open-ended add-ons. Volume breaks at 1,500 / 5,000 / 15,000 units — the quote shows all three tiers so you can decide before production starts.

cta.heading: Ready to start in step 01?
cta.description: Send us brief and scope and we'll book a kick-off session. Within 48 hours we'll return with a timeline proposal and a first meeting.

meta.title: Request a quote
meta.description: Describe the project — we'll come back with a concrete proposal within 48 hours. No generic quote, no automated reply.

hero.heading: Describe the project.
hero.description: Walk through the form in three steps — contact, project, files. No need for a perfect spec up front; we'll follow up in scoping if anything is missing.

placeholders.name: First Last
placeholders.materials: e.g. FSC kraft 180 gsm, matte lamination
placeholders.notes: Brand references, prior work, delivery location — anything that helps us understand the brief.

validation.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
validation.phoneInvalid: That phone number doesn't look right.
validation.websiteInvalid: URL must start with http:// or https://.
validation.quantityInteger: Quantity must be a whole number.

contact.label: Contact
contact.heading: Who are you?
contact.description: Your contact and company details — so we know who to come back to.

project.label: Project
project.heading: What are we building?
project.description: As much detail as you have on product, volume and timing. Blank fields are fine — we'll follow up in scoping.

files.label: Files & notes
files.heading: Share brief & details.
files.description: Attach brief, mood board or prior work. Max 5 files, 10 MB each. Strictly confidential — NDA available on request.

fileUploader.dropzoneHeading: Drop files here, or click to choose.
fileUploader.dropzoneHint: PDF, PNG, JPG, AI, EPS — max 10 MB per file. Briefs, mood boards, logotypes, prior work.

rejection.type: File type not supported. Allowed: PDF, PNG, JPG, WEBP, AI, EPS.
rejection.count: Maximum of 5 files at a time.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Try again or call us direct.
errors.network: Couldn't reach the server. Check your connection and try again.

success.heading: Thanks — we'll be back within 48 hours.
success.body: We've received your quote request. A confirmation with the summary is on its way to your inbox. If anything needs clarification we'll reach out directly.

sidebar.heading: Four steps to a quote.
sidebar.description: Quote requests are handled personally — no automated reply.

response.title: Response within 48 hours
response.description: We review brief and scope personally, usually the same day during office hours.

proposal.title: Concrete proposal
proposal.description: Fixed quote with all volume breaks, lead time and delivery details — no template.

scoping.title: Scoping call when needed
scoping.description: 30 minutes to walk through the brief if anything needs filling in.

nda.title: NDA available
nda.description: We sign a mutual NDA before the brief — your template or ours.

meta.title: Contact
meta.description: Contact AW AB in Stockholm — a discreet conversation within 48 hours, no boilerplate pitch.

hero.heading: Let's talk.
hero.description: Write a few lines about what you need, and we'll come back within 48 hours. For urgent projects — call direct, it's faster than email.
